titleBn: Find My Device : কয়েক মিনিটের মধ্য়েই পেয়ে যাবেন হারিয়ে যাওয়া ফোন, রয়েছে এই টুল
titleEn: Find My Device : কয়েক মিনিটের মধ্য়েই পেয়ে যাবেন হারিয়ে যাওয়া ফোন, রয়েছে এই টুল
contentBn:
Find My Device: বর্তমানের এই ডিজিটাল যুগে স্মার্টফোন কেবল একটি গ্যাজেট বা যন্ত্রই নয়; বরং এটি আমাদের পরিচয়, কাজ এবং ব্যক্তিগত জীবনের এক অবিচ্ছেদ্য অংশ। তাই, আপনার ফোনটি হারিয়ে গেলে বা চুরি হয়ে গেলে দুশ্চিন্তা হওয়াটা খুবই স্বাভাবিক। সৌভাগ্যবশত, প্রযুক্তির এতটাই উন্নতি হয়েছে যে আপনি এখন খুব সহজেই আপনার হারিয়ে যাওয়া ফোনটি খুঁজে পেতে পারেন। 'Find My Device' হলো এমনই একটি অনন্য ফিচার, যা এই কঠিন সময়ে আপনাকে সহায়তা করতে পারে।
'Find My Device' কী এবং এটি কীভাবে কাজ করে ?Google-এর 'Find My Device' হলো এমন একটি টুল বা মাধ্যম, যা আপনাকে আপনার স্মার্টফোনের অবস্থান (location) ট্র্যাক করতে সাহায্য করে। এই ফিচারটি আপনার ফোনের GPS, ইন্টারনেট সংযোগ এবং Google অ্যাকাউন্টের সহায়তায় কাজ করে। আপনার ফোনটি অনলাইনে সংযুক্ত হওয়ার সাথে সাথেই আপনি সেটির বর্তমান বা লাইভ অবস্থান দেখতে পাবেন।
ফোনটি খুঁজে পেতে কী কী প্রয়োজন?এই ফিচারটি ব্যবহার করার জন্য কিছু পূর্বশর্ত বা প্রয়োজনীয় বিষয় রয়েছে। প্রথমত, আপনার ফোনে অবশ্যই আপনার Google অ্যাকাউন্টে লগ-ইন করা থাকতে হবে। এছাড়া, ফোনটির ইন্টারনেট সংযোগ এবং 'Location' বা অবস্থান ট্র্যাকিং চালু (on) থাকতে হবে। যদি এই বিষয়গুলো চালু থাকে, তবে ফোনটি ট্র্যাক করা অনেক সহজ হয়ে যায়।
হারিয়ে যাওয়া ফোন কীভাবে ট্র্যাক করবেন?যদি আপনার ফোনটি হারিয়ে গিয়ে থাকে, তবে প্রথমে অন্য কোনো ডিভাইস থেকে 'Find My Device'-এর ওয়েবসাইট বা অ্যাপটি খুলুন। এরপর, আপনার Google অ্যাকাউন্ট দিয়ে লগ-ইন করুন। একবার লগ-ইন করা হয়ে গেলে, আপনি একটি মানচিত্রের (map) ওপর আপনার ফোনের অবস্থানটি দেখতে পাবেন। আপনি চাইলে আপনার ফোনটিতে জোরে রিং বা শব্দ করার নির্দেশও দিতে পারেন; এতে ফোনটি কাছাকাছি কোথাও থাকলে তা খুঁজে পাওয়া আরও সহজ হয়ে যায়। মজার ব্যাপার হলো, ফোনটি 'সাইলেন্ট মোড'-এ থাকলেও এই রিং বা শব্দ করার সুবিধাটি কাজ করে।
ডেটা বা তথ্য সুরক্ষিত রাখতে কী করবেন?যদি আপনার মনে হয় যে ফোনটি ফেরত পাওয়া কঠিন, তবে আপনি এই ফিচারটি ব্যবহার করে আপনার ফোনটি লক বা তালাবদ্ধ করে দিতে পারেন। এর ফলে অন্য কেউ আপনার ফোনে থাকা ডেটা বা তথ্যের নাগাল পাবে না। প্রয়োজনে, আপনি দূর থেকেই (remotely) আপনার ফোনের সমস্ত ডেটা মুছে ফেলতে পারেন; এর মাধ্যমে আপনার ব্যক্তিগত তথ্যের নিরাপত্তা নিশ্চিত করা সম্ভব।
কোন বিষয়গুলো মনে রাখা প্রয়োজন?'Find My Device' ফিচারটি কেবল তখনই কাজ করে, যখন ফোনটি চালু থাকে এবং ইন্টারনেটের সাথে সংযুক্ত থাকে। যদি ফোনটি বন্ধ (switched off) থাকে, তবে কেবল সেটির সর্বশেষ অবস্থানটিই প্রদর্শন করা হয়। তাই, আপনার ফোনের 'Location' এবং ইন্টারনেট সংযোগ সর্বদা চালু রাখাটাই সবচেয়ে বুদ্ধিমানের কাজ। এছাড়াও, কেউ যাতে আপনার তথ্যের অপব্যবহার করতে না পারে, সেজন্য আপনার Google অ্যাকাউন্টের পাসওয়ার্ড সুরক্ষিত রাখুন এবং 'টু-স্টেপ ভেরিফিকেশন' চালু রাখুন।
contentEn:
Find My Device: বর্তমানের এই ডিজিটাল যুগে স্মার্টফোন কেবল একটি গ্যাজেট বা যন্ত্রই নয়; বরং এটি আমাদের পরিচয়, কাজ এবং ব্যক্তিগত জীবনের এক অবিচ্ছেদ্য অংশ। তাই, আপনার ফোনটি হারিয়ে গেলে বা চুরি হয়ে গেলে দুশ্চিন্তা হওয়াটা খুবই স্বাভাবিক। সৌভাগ্যবশত, প্রযুক্তির এতটাই উন্নতি হয়েছে যে আপনি এখন খুব সহজেই আপনার হারিয়ে যাওয়া ফোনটি খুঁজে পেতে পারেন। 'Find My Device' হলো এমনই একটি অনন্য ফিচার, যা এই কঠিন সময়ে আপনাকে সহায়তা করতে পারে।
'Find My Device' কী এবং এটি কীভাবে কাজ করে ?Google-এর 'Find My Device' হলো এমন একটি টুল বা মাধ্যম, যা আপনাকে আপনার স্মার্টফোনের অবস্থান (location) ট্র্যাক করতে সাহায্য করে। এই ফিচারটি আপনার ফোনের GPS, ইন্টারনেট সংযোগ এবং Google অ্যাকাউন্টের সহায়তায় কাজ করে। আপনার ফোনটি অনলাইনে সংযুক্ত হওয়ার সাথে সাথেই আপনি সেটির বর্তমান বা লাইভ অবস্থান দেখতে পাবেন।
ফোনটি খুঁজে পেতে কী কী প্রয়োজন?এই ফিচারটি ব্যবহার করার জন্য কিছু পূর্বশর্ত বা প্রয়োজনীয় বিষয় রয়েছে। প্রথমত, আপনার ফোনে অবশ্যই আপনার Google অ্যাকাউন্টে লগ-ইন করা থাকতে হবে। এছাড়া, ফোনটির ইন্টারনেট সংযোগ এবং 'Location' বা অবস্থান ট্র্যাকিং চালু (on) থাকতে হবে। যদি এই বিষয়গুলো চালু থাকে, তবে ফোনটি ট্র্যাক করা অনেক সহজ হয়ে যায়।
হারিয়ে যাওয়া ফোন কীভাবে ট্র্যাক করবেন?যদি আপনার ফোনটি হারিয়ে গিয়ে থাকে, তবে প্রথমে অন্য কোনো ডিভাইস থেকে 'Find My Device'-এর ওয়েবসাইট বা অ্যাপটি খুলুন। এরপর, আপনার Google অ্যাকাউন্ট দিয়ে লগ-ইন করুন। একবার লগ-ইন করা হয়ে গেলে, আপনি একটি মানচিত্রের (map) ওপর আপনার ফোনের অবস্থানটি দেখতে পাবেন। আপনি চাইলে আপনার ফোনটিতে জোরে রিং বা শব্দ করার নির্দেশও দিতে পারেন; এতে ফোনটি কাছাকাছি কোথাও থাকলে তা খুঁজে পাওয়া আরও সহজ হয়ে যায়। মজার ব্যাপার হলো, ফোনটি 'সাইলেন্ট মোড'-এ থাকলেও এই রিং বা শব্দ করার সুবিধাটি কাজ করে।
ডেটা বা তথ্য সুরক্ষিত রাখতে কী করবেন?যদি আপনার মনে হয় যে ফোনটি ফেরত পাওয়া কঠিন, তবে আপনি এই ফিচারটি ব্যবহার করে আপনার ফোনটি লক বা তালাবদ্ধ করে দিতে পারেন। এর ফলে অন্য কেউ আপনার ফোনে থাকা ডেটা বা তথ্যের নাগাল পাবে না। প্রয়োজনে, আপনি দূর থেকেই (remotely) আপনার ফোনের সমস্ত ডেটা মুছে ফেলতে পারেন; এর মাধ্যমে আপনার ব্যক্তিগত তথ্যের নিরাপত্তা নিশ্চিত করা সম্ভব।
কোন বিষয়গুলো মনে রাখা প্রয়োজন?'Find My Device' ফিচারটি কেবল তখনই কাজ করে, যখন ফোনটি চালু থাকে এবং ইন্টারনেটের সাথে সংযুক্ত থাকে। যদি ফোনটি বন্ধ (switched off) থাকে, তবে কেবল সেটির সর্বশেষ অবস্থানটিই প্রদর্শন করা হয়। তাই, আপনার ফোনের 'Location' এবং ইন্টারনেট সংযোগ সর্বদা চালু রাখাটাই সবচেয়ে বুদ্ধিমানের কাজ। এছাড়াও, কেউ যাতে আপনার তথ্যের অপব্যবহার করতে না পারে, সেজন্য আপনার Google অ্যাকাউন্টের পাসওয়ার্ড সুরক্ষিত রাখুন এবং 'টু-স্টেপ ভেরিফিকেশন' চালু রাখুন।

titleBn: Americans share the same struggles despite deep political divides, new bipartisan report finds
titleEn: Americans share the same struggles despite deep political divides, new bipartisan report finds
contentBn: As the United States approaches its 250th anniversary amid deep political division, a major new bipartisan report aims to get consensus on a fundamental question: How are we really doing as a country? The new "State of the States" report, released by the State of the Nation Project at Tulane University, has analyzed more than three decades of data for all 50 states and the District of Columbia, to provide a long-term progress report for each state.
contentEn: As the United States approaches its 250th anniversary amid deep political division, a major new bipartisan report aims to get consensus on a fundamental question: How are we really doing as a country? The new "State of the States" report, released by the State of the Nation Project at Tulane University, has analyzed more than three decades of data for all 50 states and the District of Columbia, to provide a long-term progress report for each state.

titleBn: Scientists map more than 200 years of nature's progress
titleEn: Scientists map more than 200 years of nature's progress
contentBn: Armed with trail cameras, artificial intelligence, and a powerful national research network, scientists are revisiting Lewis and Clark's legendary journey to see how America's wildlife has changed over the past 200 years.
contentEn: Armed with trail cameras, artificial intelligence, and a powerful national research network, scientists are revisiting Lewis and Clark's legendary journey to see how America's wildlife has changed over the past 200 years.

titleBn: A new strategy for assembling π-conjugated panels into square molecules revealed
titleEn: A new strategy for assembling π-conjugated panels into square molecules revealed
contentBn: A research group has developed a new method for selectively synthesizing three-dimensional macrocycles,⁽¹⁾ in which four panels are arranged in a square, by connecting planar π-conjugated molecules⁽²⁾ at right angles.
contentEn: A research group has developed a new method for selectively synthesizing three-dimensional macrocycles,⁽¹⁾ in which four panels are arranged in a square, by connecting planar π-conjugated molecules⁽²⁾ at right angles.

titleBn: Starting kindergarten soon? Summer is a perfect time to support a child's early literacy learning
titleEn: Starting kindergarten soon? Summer is a perfect time to support a child's early literacy learning
contentBn: The first day of kindergarten is a momentous occasion for children and families. It's an exciting milestone that comes with new friends, teachers, and learning opportunities. It can also bring parental anxiety about whether their child is ready, especially when it comes to early literacy.
contentEn: The first day of kindergarten is a momentous occasion for children and families. It's an exciting milestone that comes with new friends, teachers, and learning opportunities. It can also bring parental anxiety about whether their child is ready, especially when it comes to early literacy.

titleBn: Why do rival plants coexist? The secret is in the soil beneath the oaks
titleEn: Why do rival plants coexist? The secret is in the soil beneath the oaks
contentBn: How can plants that compete for the same resources grow in the same area without one driving the other to extinction? Ecologists have been trying to answer this question for decades, and a surprising new explanation has now emerged: the soil surrounding oak trees acts as a silent mediator that restrains the dominant species and gives an advantage to weaker ones, allowing both to coexist.
contentEn: How can plants that compete for the same resources grow in the same area without one driving the other to extinction? Ecologists have been trying to answer this question for decades, and a surprising new explanation has now emerged: the soil surrounding oak trees acts as a silent mediator that restrains the dominant species and gives an advantage to weaker ones, allowing both to coexist.

titleBn: Transnational history explores the Japanese migration to Canada 1877–1988
titleEn: Transnational history explores the Japanese migration to Canada 1877–1988
contentBn: "Japanese Migration to Canada, 1877–1988," a new reference essay by Masumi Izumi, was published in the Oxford Research Encyclopedia of Migration Studies. The article offers a sweeping, deeply researched account of Japanese migration to Canada from the arrival of the first documented migrant in 1877 through the Canadian government's formal redress settlement of 1988.
contentEn: "Japanese Migration to Canada, 1877–1988," a new reference essay by Masumi Izumi, was published in the Oxford Research Encyclopedia of Migration Studies. The article offers a sweeping, deeply researched account of Japanese migration to Canada from the arrival of the first documented migrant in 1877 through the Canadian government's formal redress settlement of 1988.

titleBn: Physicists discover attractive forces between molecular condensates may cause running off
titleEn: Physicists discover attractive forces between molecular condensates may cause running off
contentBn: Inside cells, certain functions are carried out by locally adjusting molecular composition. This condensation of material results in the formation of dense droplets that can dynamically rearrange. Because of this, interactions between such dense regions determine the shaping of condensates. Scientists from the Department of Living Matter Physics at MPI-DS recently developed a model that can describe such phase separation dynamics based solely on attraction. The work is published in the journal Physical Review Letters.
contentEn: Inside cells, certain functions are carried out by locally adjusting molecular composition. This condensation of material results in the formation of dense droplets that can dynamically rearrange. Because of this, interactions between such dense regions determine the shaping of condensates. Scientists from the Department of Living Matter Physics at MPI-DS recently developed a model that can describe such phase separation dynamics based solely on attraction. The work is published in the journal Physical Review Letters.

titleBn: Did this star eat its planets? A new study offers clues on 'chemical paradox' of a binary system
titleEn: Did this star eat its planets? A new study offers clues on 'chemical paradox' of a binary system
contentBn: Astronomers have investigated a puzzling binary star system in which two stars that may have formed together now show dramatically different chemical compositions. The new study, uploaded to the arXiv preprint server on May 29, hints at the possibility that one of the stars may have swallowed its own planets.
contentEn: Astronomers have investigated a puzzling binary star system in which two stars that may have formed together now show dramatically different chemical compositions. The new study, uploaded to the arXiv preprint server on May 29, hints at the possibility that one of the stars may have swallowed its own planets.

titleBn: Light pulses uncover Higgs mode that reshapes perovskite crystal symmetry
titleEn: Light pulses uncover Higgs mode that reshapes perovskite crystal symmetry
contentBn: Waves of light and sound interact to drive electronic and structural changes in a perovskite crystal. At the atomic scale, nothing is ever truly still. Materials that appear perfectly rigid and motionless to the naked eye are in fact swarms of vibrating atoms. This motion is generally random and uncoordinated, but with the right input, the atoms in certain materials will start to move together, vibrating in sync.
contentEn: Waves of light and sound interact to drive electronic and structural changes in a perovskite crystal. At the atomic scale, nothing is ever truly still. Materials that appear perfectly rigid and motionless to the naked eye are in fact swarms of vibrating atoms. This motion is generally random and uncoordinated, but with the right input, the atoms in certain materials will start to move together, vibrating in sync.

titleBn: Hagfish fossils reveal stepwise eye simplification before near-total vision loss
titleEn: Hagfish fossils reveal stepwise eye simplification before near-total vision loss
contentBn: Many animals, including humans, rely on their eyes to detect changes in their surroundings. The eyes of vertebrates, animals with a backbone or a similar supporting structure, contain a transparent structure (i.e., the lens) that focuses incoming light onto a layer of light-sensitive cells, known as the retina. Cells in the retina then convert light into signals that are sent to the brain.
contentEn: Many animals, including humans, rely on their eyes to detect changes in their surroundings. The eyes of vertebrates, animals with a backbone or a similar supporting structure, contain a transparent structure (i.e., the lens) that focuses incoming light onto a layer of light-sensitive cells, known as the retina. Cells in the retina then convert light into signals that are sent to the brain.

titleBn: Telehealth can improve care for cats with chronic health issues
titleEn: Telehealth can improve care for cats with chronic health issues
contentBn: Researchers found telehealth visits can improve care for cats with feline arthritis.
contentEn: Researchers found telehealth visits can improve care for cats with feline arthritis.

titleBn: Researchers develop innovative model to study sense of smell
titleEn: Researchers develop innovative model to study sense of smell
contentBn: Using a newly devised, three-dimensional model to study the regeneration of nerve tissue in the nose, researchers have discovered that one type of stem cell thought to be dormant may play a more significant role in preserving the sense of smell than originally believed.
contentEn: Using a newly devised, three-dimensional model to study the regeneration of nerve tissue in the nose, researchers have discovered that one type of stem cell thought to be dormant may play a more significant role in preserving the sense of smell than originally believed.

titleBn: Collaboration can unlock Australia's energy transition without sacrificing natural capital
titleEn: Collaboration can unlock Australia's energy transition without sacrificing natural capital
contentBn: New research demonstrates that with collaboration between stakeholders, Australia can fully decarbonize its domestic and energy export economies by 2060 -- a feat requiring $6.2 trillion USD and around 110,000 square kilomters of land -- while avoiding harm to important areas for biodiversity outcomes, safeguarding agricultural activities, and respecting Indigenous land rights.
contentEn: New research demonstrates that with collaboration between stakeholders, Australia can fully decarbonize its domestic and energy export economies by 2060 -- a feat requiring $6.2 trillion USD and around 110,000 square kilomters of land -- while avoiding harm to important areas for biodiversity outcomes, safeguarding agricultural activities, and respecting Indigenous land rights.

titleBn: Decades-old assumptions about brain plasticity upended
titleEn: Decades-old assumptions about brain plasticity upended
contentBn: A new study challenges a decades-old assumption in neuroscience by showing that the brain uses distinct transmission sites -- not a shared site -- to achieve different types of plasticity.
contentEn: A new study challenges a decades-old assumption in neuroscience by showing that the brain uses distinct transmission sites -- not a shared site -- to achieve different types of plasticity.

titleBn: Guardrails, education urged to protect adolescent AI users
titleEn: Guardrails, education urged to protect adolescent AI users
contentBn: The effects of artificial intelligence on adolescents are nuanced and complex, according to a new report that calls on developers to prioritize features that protect young people from exploitation, manipulation and the erosion of real-world relationships.
contentEn: The effects of artificial intelligence on adolescents are nuanced and complex, according to a new report that calls on developers to prioritize features that protect young people from exploitation, manipulation and the erosion of real-world relationships.

titleBn: Eating an array of smaller fish could be nutrient-dense solution to overfishing
titleEn: Eating an array of smaller fish could be nutrient-dense solution to overfishing
contentBn: To satisfy the seafood needs of billions of people, offering them access to a more biodiverse array of fish creates opportunities to mix-and-match species to obtain better nutrition from smaller portions of fish.
contentEn: To satisfy the seafood needs of billions of people, offering them access to a more biodiverse array of fish creates opportunities to mix-and-match species to obtain better nutrition from smaller portions of fish.

titleBn: Molecular link between air pollution and pregnancy risks
titleEn: Molecular link between air pollution and pregnancy risks
contentBn: A new study found exposure to specific tiny particles in air pollution during pregnancy are associated with increased risk of various negative birth outcomes.
contentEn: A new study found exposure to specific tiny particles in air pollution during pregnancy are associated with increased risk of various negative birth outcomes.

titleBn: Pancreatic cancer spreads to liver or lung thanks to this protein
titleEn: Pancreatic cancer spreads to liver or lung thanks to this protein
contentBn: Scientists have discovered how pancreatic cancer cells thrive in the lungs or liver, environments that are as distinct to cells as the ocean and desert are to animals. The spread of cancer cells to organs like these often produces the very first symptoms of pancreatic cancer. But by that time, the pancreatic cancer has spread out of control.
contentEn: Scientists have discovered how pancreatic cancer cells thrive in the lungs or liver, environments that are as distinct to cells as the ocean and desert are to animals. The spread of cancer cells to organs like these often produces the very first symptoms of pancreatic cancer. But by that time, the pancreatic cancer has spread out of control.

titleBn: Epilepsy is more common in patients with frontotemporal dementia than expected
titleEn: Epilepsy is more common in patients with frontotemporal dementia than expected
contentBn: According to a recent study, in patients with frontotemporal dementia (FTD), epileptic seizures are significantly more common than previously known. The discovery deepens understanding of the symptoms of this memory disorder and emphasises the importance of taking epileptic seizures into account in the treatment and monitoring of patients.
contentEn: According to a recent study, in patients with frontotemporal dementia (FTD), epileptic seizures are significantly more common than previously known. The discovery deepens understanding of the symptoms of this memory disorder and emphasises the importance of taking epileptic seizures into account in the treatment and monitoring of patients.

titleBn: Millions of new solar system objects to be found and 'filmed in technicolor' -- studies predict
titleEn: Millions of new solar system objects to be found and 'filmed in technicolor' -- studies predict
contentBn: Astronomers have revealed new research showing that millions of new solar system objects are likely to be detected by a brand-new facility, which is expected to come online later this year.
contentEn: Astronomers have revealed new research showing that millions of new solar system objects are likely to be detected by a brand-new facility, which is expected to come online later this year.

titleBn: Ebola scare in India: Three African nationals quarantined for 21-days in Chhattisgarh's Durg
titleEn: Ebola scare in India: Three African nationals quarantined for 21-days in Chhattisgarh's Durg
contentBn: Chhattisgarh has reported three suspected Ebola cases involving foreign nationals quarantined in Durg as a precautionary measure. State health minister assures preparedness, with hospitals and facilities on alert. This heightened vigilance follows WHO's classification of outbreaks in Uganda and DRC as a Public Health Emergency of International Concern.
contentEn: Chhattisgarh has reported three suspected Ebola cases involving foreign nationals quarantined in Durg as a precautionary measure. State health minister assures preparedness, with hospitals and facilities on alert. This heightened vigilance follows WHO's classification of outbreaks in Uganda and DRC as a Public Health Emergency of International Concern.

titleBn: 'Dharmendra Pradhan must go': Cockroach Janta Party gather at Jantar Manatar - top developments
titleEn: 'Dharmendra Pradhan must go': Cockroach Janta Party gather at Jantar Manatar - top developments
contentBn: The protest attracted hundreds of participants, most of them students and young professionals. Many attendees wore cockroach masks and carried flowers, while schoolchildren were also seen at the venue accompanied by their parents. Protesters raised slogans and called for the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over issues linked to examinations such as NEET, CBSE, CUET and SSC.
contentEn: The protest attracted hundreds of participants, most of them students and young professionals. Many attendees wore cockroach masks and carried flowers, while schoolchildren were also seen at the venue accompanied by their parents. Protesters raised slogans and called for the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over issues linked to examinations such as NEET, CBSE, CUET and SSC.

titleEn: Banned antibiotics behind 43% of shrimp export rejections; CDSCO seeks stricter enforcement

titleBn: 'Any sanctions threat would boomerang': Putin hails India's stance against foreign diktats
titleEn: 'Any sanctions threat would boomerang': Putin hails India's stance against foreign diktats
contentBn: The Russian president said Kremlin's cooperation with India "is not subject to the political environment" and that Moscow would stand "true to commitments given to our partners, especially partners like India". "Our cooperation with India, just like with all other partners of ours, is not subject to the political environment. We can't be dictated to not deliver that to India. Nobody can dictate to us and nobody would even try to do that... We will always stand true to commitments given to our partners, especially partners like India," he further said.
contentEn: The Russian president said Kremlin's cooperation with India "is not subject to the political environment" and that Moscow would stand "true to commitments given to our partners, especially partners like India". "Our cooperation with India, just like with all other partners of ours, is not subject to the political environment. We can't be dictated to not deliver that to India. Nobody can dictate to us and nobody would even try to do that... We will always stand true to commitments given to our partners, especially partners like India," he further said.

titleBn: AI reshaping exercise of judicial and sovereign powers: CJI
titleEn: AI reshaping exercise of judicial and sovereign powers: CJI
contentBn: In a recent address, Chief Justice Surya Kant urged the formation of an international legal framework to manage the transnational implications of AI technology. He warned that AI disrupts traditional notions of jurisdiction and accountability, giving rise to a troubling accountability deficit, particularly for self-governing systems.
contentEn: In a recent address, Chief Justice Surya Kant urged the formation of an international legal framework to manage the transnational implications of AI technology. He warned that AI disrupts traditional notions of jurisdiction and accountability, giving rise to a troubling accountability deficit, particularly for self-governing systems.

titleBn: 'If they are arrested': Sonam Wangchuk warns of six-week fast ahead of CJP protest
titleEn: 'If they are arrested': Sonam Wangchuk warns of six-week fast ahead of CJP protest
contentBn: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk will begin a 42-day hunger strike if members of the Cockroach Janta Party are arrested ahead of their Jantar Mantar protest. Wangchuk, traveling from Ladakh, urged supporters to maintain a peaceful demonstration, warning against provocations that could tarnish the youth-led movement. The protest seeks the education minister's resignation over examination irregularities.
contentEn: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk will begin a 42-day hunger strike if members of the Cockroach Janta Party are arrested ahead of their Jantar Mantar protest. Wangchuk, traveling from Ladakh, urged supporters to maintain a peaceful demonstration, warning against provocations that could tarnish the youth-led movement. The protest seeks the education minister's resignation over examination irregularities.

titleBn: 'J&K was, is and will remain part of India': New Delhi rebukes Pakistan at UN
titleEn: 'J&K was, is and will remain part of India': New Delhi rebukes Pakistan at UN
contentBn: At the UNGA, India took a bold stand against Pakistan, critiquing its manipulation of UN discussions for political leverage. The Indian envoy reaffirmed that Jammu and Kashmir is a core component of India, calling out Pakistan's attempts to distort facts as flimsy and ineffective.
contentEn: At the UNGA, India took a bold stand against Pakistan, critiquing its manipulation of UN discussions for political leverage. The Indian envoy reaffirmed that Jammu and Kashmir is a core component of India, calling out Pakistan's attempts to distort facts as flimsy and ineffective.

titleBn: CBSE extends post-result application deadline after students flag portal issues
titleEn: CBSE extends post-result application deadline after students flag portal issues
contentBn: CBSE has extended the deadline for Class 12 answer sheet verification and re-evaluation applications to June 7 due to portal access issues. This decision offers students more time to address concerns about scanned copies and evaluation discrepancies. The board previously opened its portal for students to report issues like missing pages or incorrect answer sheets.
contentEn: CBSE has extended the deadline for Class 12 answer sheet verification and re-evaluation applications to June 7 due to portal access issues. This decision offers students more time to address concerns about scanned copies and evaluation discrepancies. The board previously opened its portal for students to report issues like missing pages or incorrect answer sheets.

titleBn: RBI keeps repo rate unchanged at 5.25%: What it means for real estate sector
titleEn: RBI keeps repo rate unchanged at 5.25%: What it means for real estate sector
contentBn: The Reserve Bank of India maintained its repo rate at 5.25%, bringing stability to the real estate sector. This steady policy is boosting buyer confidence and aiding developers' financial planning. Experts believe the unchanged stance supports housing demand and affordability, fostering a predictable environment for long-term investments amidst economic uncertainties.

This steady policy is boosting buyer confidence and aiding developers' financial planning. Experts believe the unchanged stance supports housing demand and affordability, fostering a predictable environment for long-term investments amidst economic uncertainties.

titleBn: From Project-75I to Project-76: What PM's Hazira visit reveals about India's submarine plans
titleEn: From Project-75I to Project-76: What PM's Hazira visit reveals about India's submarine plans
contentBn: India is boosting its submarine fleet with indigenous Project-76 submarines, aiming to counter a significant shortfall. Despite past delays, the Navy is progressing with both conventional and nuclear submarine programs. The new AIP-equipped submarines promise enhanced underwater endurance, crucial for national security and power projection in the Indo-Pacific region.
contentEn: India is boosting its submarine fleet with indigenous Project-76 submarines, aiming to counter a significant shortfall. Despite past delays, the Navy is progressing with both conventional and nuclear submarine programs. The new AIP-equipped submarines promise enhanced underwater endurance, crucial for national security and power projection in the Indo-Pacific region.

titleBn: Bill aims to end H-1B route to Green Cards & cut OPT: What the new act proposes
titleEn: Bill aims to end H-1B route to Green Cards & cut OPT: What the new act proposes
contentBn: U.S. Representative Chip Roy has introduced the "American White-Collar Worker Jobs Act of 2026" to reform the H-1B visa program. The bill aims to end H-1B visas as a path to permanent residency and eliminate the Optional Practical Training (OPT) program. It seeks to prioritize American STEM professionals by enforcing stricter wage standards and preventing displacement of U.S. workers.
contentEn: U.S. Representative Chip Roy has introduced the "American White-Collar Worker Jobs Act of 2026" to reform the H-1B visa program. The bill aims to end H-1B visas as a path to permanent residency and eliminate the Optional Practical Training (OPT) program. It seeks to prioritize American STEM professionals by enforcing stricter wage standards and preventing displacement of U.S. workers.

titleBn: Anthropic publishes 10,000 word paper suggesting AI can be more dangerous than job cuts
titleEn: Anthropic publishes 10,000 word paper suggesting AI can be more dangerous than job cuts
contentBn: Anthropic has published a 10,000-plus word paper arguing that the real AI danger isn't just job losses, the warning CEO Dario Amodei has been repeating all year. It's recursive self-improvement: AI systems capable of designing and training their own successors. The paper reveals Claude now writes over 80% of Anthropic's code, and floats a coordinated pause on frontier AI development if rival labs agree to verifiably do the same.
contentEn: Anthropic has published a 10,000-plus word paper arguing that the real AI danger isn't just job losses, the warning CEO Dario Amodei has been repeating all year. It's recursive self-improvement: AI systems capable of designing and training their own successors. The paper reveals Claude now writes over 80% of Anthropic's code, and floats a coordinated pause on frontier AI development if rival labs agree to verifiably do the same.

titleEn: CBSE Class 12 Re-Evaluation Portal Fails to Launch on June 1, Students Left Waiting
contentBn: CBSE faces delays as the Class 12 re-evaluation portal remains offline; eligible candidates can challenge marks for specific questions via the On-Screen Marking system with scanned copies.

titleBn: AP EAMCET Results 2026: Check Scorecards At cets.apsche.ap.gov.in; Steps To Download Rank Card
titleEn: AP EAMCET Results 2026: Check Scorecards At cets.apsche.ap.gov.in; Steps To Download Rank Card
contentBn: AP EAMCET Results 2026 will be announced on June 1 on the official APSCHE portal. Candidates can download their scorecards using login credentials, while counselling, document verification and seat allotment processes will begin for qualified candidates after the results are declared.
